metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3** — Add `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` to S3's named edit targets and explicitly state the required change: replace `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` with a `build:` directive pointing at the new `infra/compose/Dockerfile.gerrit`.
findings.finding: The `docker-compose.yml` uses `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (a bare pull) for the Gerrit service, but S3 requires building a custom image (`FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 + Python ≥3.11 + pip install gerrit-to-platform`). Switching from `image:` to `build:` in docker-compose.yml is a required edit that is not named as an edit target anywhere in the plan (S3 names `infra/compose/Dockerfile.gerrit` as new, but never mentions `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`).
findings.location: S3 — g2p install; infra/compose/docker-compose.yml line 28; Deployment sequencing 'New file consumers' list
findings.suggested_fix: In S3's AC, add: 'Update `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`: replace `image: gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` with `build: { context: ../.., dockerfile: infra/compose/Dockerfile.gerrit }`.' Also add `docker-compose.yml` to the 'New file consumers' list in Deployment sequencing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `github_actions_secret` resource (or equivalent) to `infra/terraform-github/main.tf` for `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` (and `GERRIT_*` variables) so the CI SSH key has an IaC-tracked lifecycle with a defined rotation/retire path — consistent with the SSM pattern used for the PAT.
findings.finding: The CI service-account SSH private key (GitHub Actions secret GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY) is not tracked by any IaC. The plan says 'add the SSH key + PAT slots to ssm.tf', but GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY is stored as a GitHub Actions secret — not an SSM parameter — and the infra/terraform-github module has no github_actions_secret resource. This creates a long-lived private key that is provisioned manually with no IaC audit trail, no rotation enforcement, and no state-tracked lifecycle.
findings.location: S4 AC — 'set GitHub repo vars … and secret GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY'; infra/terraform-github/main.tf (no github_actions_secret resource)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to infra/terraform-github/main.tf: `resource "github_actions_secret" "gerrit_ssh_privkey" { repository = var.repository; secret_name = "GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY"; encrypted_value = var.gerrit_ssh_privkey_encrypted }` with a sensitive variable and the same NEVER-commit discipline already applied to github_token. Add rotation + retire runbook steps mirroring the replication deploy-key lifecycle in ADR-0010.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit image rollback path to S6 (e.g., revert to the pinned base `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` tag, document how to rebuild/redeploy the prior image, and pin the `pip install gerrit-to-platform==<version>` to a specific release so the rollback is reproducible).
findings.finding: The extended Gerrit container image (`FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` + Python + g2p) is a destructive change to the running Gerrit service with no stated rollback path for the image itself. S6's back-out covers only the submit-requirement gate; it does not address how to recover if the extended image fails to build, boots into a broken state, or introduces a Gerrit regression.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install; S6 — Monitoring, back-out, E2E proof
findings.suggested_fix: In S3, pin `pip install gerrit-to-platform==<exact-version>` and record the prior image digest. In S6's back-out section, add: 'To roll back the container image, revert the Dockerfile to `FROM gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (no extension), redeploy, and confirm Gerrit starts cleanly; the Verified submit requirement will simply have no voter (fail-closed, no submits blocked beyond current state).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to the `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid in S5: replace 'Decide test.yml convergence (recommend…)' with a concrete decision stating the target file path (e.g., `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` with `workflow_dispatch` + `push`/`pull_request` triggers) and an observable done-criterion (the file exists and the `on:` block lists both trigger types).
findings.finding: S5's acceptance criterion says 'Decide test.yml convergence (recommend the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid…)' — the word 'Decide' combined with 'recommend' defers a real design choice (one workflow file vs two) to the executor rather than committing to it in the plan.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (AC bullet, last sentence)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the sentence with: 'Adopt the hybrid: `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` carries both `on: workflow_dispatch` (for g2p) and `on: push/pull_request` triggers (replacing `test.yml` for branch runs); `test.yml` is deleted or reduced to a redirect. Done when the file exists with both trigger types and all former `test.yml` matrix jobs pass on a branch push.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harden S5's AC to lock the convergence decision: either (a) require a single `gerrit-verify.yaml` that also handles `push`/`workflow_dispatch`-manual triggers (the recommended hybrid), making `test.yml` an alias or removing it, OR (b) require that `gerrit-verify.yaml` calls `test.yml` steps via a reusable workflow (`uses: ./.github/workflows/test.yml`) — and add a CI check or comment block that enforces the canonical-source rule so the two cannot silently diverge.
findings.finding: The convergence of `gerrit-verify.yaml` CI steps with `test.yml` is explicitly left as an open decision ('Decide test.yml convergence') in S5 — if the two files diverge (duplicate step definitions rather than reuse), CI rules for Gerrit-triggered runs and branch/manual runs can drift independently, creating a hidden maintainability hazard across the GitHub Actions ↔ Gerrit integration boundary.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow / last sentence of the bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S3 (or the g2p adoption ADR), specify the tracked repo path for `gerrit_to_platform.ini` (e.g., `infra/gerrit/gerrit_to_platform.ini.tmpl` with a `${GERRIT_TO_PLATFORM_PAT}` placeholder), the mechanism that renders it at container startup (e.g., `entrypoint.sh` substitution from container env), and note that the rendered file must not be volume-mounted from the host or committed with a real token.
findings.finding: `gerrit_to_platform.ini` is mentioned as something to 'write' in S3 but the plan never specifies its tracked location (in-repo path vs. runtime-generated), whether it is version-controlled or produced by a start-up script, or which document covers its structure and the env-var substitution pattern for the PAT.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install; Secrets & credentials section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **T12 — Per-layer rollback:** Add explicit back-out steps for S2 (revert the `refs/changes/*` refspec from `replication.config` and restart replication plugin) and S3 (maintain a tagged pre-extension image reference so `docker compose` can be reverted to the previous image in one command) in addition to the S6 submit-requirement rollback.
findings.finding: The S6 back-out procedure covers only the Verified submit-requirement layer (re-enabling single-vote gating), but does not specify rollback for the upstream infrastructure changes — the extended Gerrit container image (S3) and the replication broadening (S2) — which are also live system changes that could independently break Gerrit startup or replication behavior before S6 is ever reached.
findings.location: S6 — Monitoring, back-out, E2E proof + docs; Deployment sequencing & risk
findings.suggested_fix: In S2 AC, add: 'Document rollback: remove the `refs/changes/*` refspec line and run `ssh gerrit plugin reload replication`.' In S3 AC, add: 'Tag the current image as `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1-pre-g2p` before building the extended image; rollback = update compose to reference the pre-g2p tag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S3, specify the exact runtime mechanism for generating `gerrit_to_platform.ini` from the container env var (e.g., an entrypoint script that uses `envsubst` on a committed template containing `${GITHUB_PAT_PLACEHOLDER}`, or a Python snippet that reads `os.environ['G2P_GITHUB_PAT']`), and confirm the committed template contains only a placeholder — never the real value.
findings.finding: The write mechanism for gerrit_to_platform.ini is underspecified with respect to secrets hygiene. The plan states 'PAT via the container-env path from Secrets — no plaintext PAT in gerrit_to_platform.ini on disk', but does not specify how the ini file is generated at container start (entrypoint envsubst? Python script reading env? committed template with placeholder?). Without a concrete mechanism, an implementer may write the PAT value directly into the committed ini template or into the image, violating the no-plaintext-in-IaC invariant.
findings.location: S3 AC — 'write gerrit_to_platform.ini (PAT via the container-env path from Secrets)'; Locked design section — 'no plaintext PAT in gerrit_to_platform.ini on disk'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S6, define the concrete metric contract for g2p/Verified alarms before implementation: specify the journald source (container name or unit), the log pattern to grep (e.g., a structured g2p error marker), the CloudWatch namespace + metric name + dimensions, mirroring the explicit contract in monitoring_s4b.tf's block comment.
findings.finding: The S6 CloudWatch alarm for g2p hook-dispatch and Verified-vote failures lacks a concrete metric contract. The plan says alarms are 'modelled on monitoring_s4b.tf + infra/scripts/observability.sh (publish a metric from a journald log pattern)' and 'name the log source + pattern + metric', but does not itself name them. The g2p process runs inside the Gerrit container, so its journald unit/container name, the log pattern to grep, the CloudWatch namespace, and the metric name are all unspecified — leaving S6 incompletely defined and risking a metric contract mismatch (alarm watches a metric the probe never publishes).
findings.location: S6 AC — 'add a CloudWatch alarm for g2p hook-dispatch failures and Verified-vote failures — detection modelled on monitoring_s4b.tf + infra/scripts/observability.sh (publish a metric from a journald log pattern, as voter_errors does; name the log source + pattern + metric)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the design-notes criterion observable: name the target artifact (e.g., a section in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` or the S6 ADR) and the minimum required headings ('Cost & latency', 'Flaky-test handling', 'copyCondition re-run behavior') so done-ness is verifiable by file inspection.
findings.finding: S6's acceptance criterion includes 'capture design notes (cost / latency budget, flaky-test handling, copyCondition re-run behavior)' — this is effort-framed (no named artifact, no target document, no definition of 'captured') and therefore not observable or verifiable.
findings.location: S6 — Monitoring, back-out, E2E proof + docs (AC bullet, third sentence)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'capture design notes (cost / latency budget, flaky-test handling, copyCondition re-run behavior)' with: 'Add a "Operational notes" section to `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` covering: (a) GitHub Actions cost at current commit frequency, (b) p95 end-to-end latency target (hook→vote), (c) flaky-test retry policy, (d) copyCondition re-run behavior on trivially-rebased patchsets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the workflow filename before implementation: pick ONE canonical name (e.g. `gerrit-verify.yaml`) and use it consistently in the Locked Design, S5 AC, and any S6 references; if the hybrid-dispatch variant is adopted, update the Locked Design to reflect the chosen name.
findings.finding: The Locked Design names the CI workflow file `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, but S5 AC introduces a different filename — `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` — as a recommendation, leaving the actual deliverable filename unresolved; an implementer verifying S5 does not know which file to create or verify.
findings.location: Locked Design ('`.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`') vs. S5 AC ('recommend the `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml` hybrid')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3/S6** — define the g2p hook retry protocol: specify the max retry count, backoff (e.g. exponential with cap), total timeout, and the exact threshold that transitions from 'transient retry' to 'persistent failure + S6 alarm', so an executor can implement the fallback deterministically.
findings.finding: The plan states the g2p hook should 'retry' transient `workflow_dispatch` failures and then alert (S6), but specifies neither the retry count, backoff interval, timeout ceiling, nor the criterion that distinguishes a 'transient' failure (worth retrying) from a 'persistent' one (triggers the alarm). This is a fallback sub-step with ambiguous placement and no operationalized specification.
findings.location: Locked design — 'Re-run' paragraph; Deployment sequencing — 'Failure surfacing' paragraph; S6 AC
findings.suggested_fix: Example specification: 'retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff (2 s, 4 s, 8 s); after 3 failures emit the CloudWatch metric and exit non-zero (leaving Verified=0); distinguish 5xx/network errors (transient) from 4xx/bad-PAT (persistent, alert immediately on first failure).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S5, explicitly specify the `concurrency` group key as `gerrit-verify-${{ inputs.GERRIT_CHANGE_NUMBER }}-${{ inputs.GERRIT_PATCHSET_NUMBER }}` (or equivalent) so that: (a) a new patchset cancels the prior patchset's run, (b) a recheck on the *same* patchset also cancels any in-progress run for that patchset, and (c) different changes are never mutually cancelling.
findings.finding: The `concurrency: cancel-in-progress` setting is described only as cancelling runs when 'a new patchset' arrives, but the `comment-added` hook (recheck) also fires `workflow_dispatch` for the *same* patchset. If the concurrency key is not scoped to patchset number (e.g., keyed on `GERRIT_CHANGE_NUMBER + GERRIT_PATCHSET_NUMBER`), a rapid double-`recheck` comment would cancel the first re-run mid-flight, potentially looping without ever completing — or conversely, if keyed too broadly, it could cancel an in-progress run for a *different* patchset of the same change.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow; Locked design — Re-run section
findings.suggested_fix: concurrency:\n  group: gerrit-verify-${{ inputs.GERRIT_CHANGE_NUMBER }}-${{ inputs.GERRIT_PATCHSET_NUMBER }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3** — Before committing the Dockerfile build approach, verify the exact enablement mechanism for the `hooks` core plugin in `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` (run `java -jar gerrit.war ls-plugins` or inspect the image), confirm whether it ships enabled by default (as `webhooks` does) or requires `init --install-plugin hooks`, and add a concrete Dockerfile `RUN` stanza + `plugins/README.md` entry for `hooks` (mirroring the `webhooks` entry).
findings.finding: The Gerrit `hooks` plugin enablement path is asserted but unverified: `install-plugins.sh` installs only `events-log.jar` + `oauth.jar`; `plugins/README.md` documents `webhooks` (bundled+auto-enabled) but never mentions `hooks` or `replication`. The plan claims both are 'core plugins — bundled in gerrit.war, enabled via init --install-plugin or copy-from-war; no external download/sha256 pin' — but no existing infrastructure file demonstrates this path has been tested, and the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image may not enable `hooks` by default (unlike `webhooks` which the README explicitly calls already-enabled).
findings.location: S3 — g2p install (AC); Decision log 'Verified facts'; infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh; infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md
findings.suggested_fix: In the extended Dockerfile, add: `RUN java -jar /var/gerrit/bin/gerrit.war init --install-plugin hooks --no-auto-start --batch -d /var/gerrit` (or the equivalent copy-from-war step). Verify it by running the image locally and checking `ls /var/gerrit/plugins/hooks.jar`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S5** — Before writing the workflow YAML, verify `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`'s README/action.yml to confirm exactly how it consumes the SSH private key (input param, env var, or pre-loaded ssh-agent), and specify the required setup steps in S5's AC — including whether a `webfactory/ssh-agent` step is needed.
findings.finding: The plan does not state whether `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` performs SSH private-key injection itself (i.e., loads `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` into an ssh-agent internally) or whether a prior workflow step such as `webfactory/ssh-agent` or `ssh-add` is required. The action's auth precondition — which SSH setup the calling workflow must provide — is unverified and unspecified in S5.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow; S4 — Service account + credentials; Locked design 'inbound GitHub→Gerrit SSH vote path'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S4/S5 (T5c — AUTHN/encryption):** Require that the `gerrit-verify.yaml` workflow's SSH invocation (via `gerrit-review-action`) enforces `StrictHostKeyChecking=yes` and loads host keys from the materialized `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` var — document this in S4's ADR and in the S5 workflow spec, mirroring the pattern already used in `infra/gerrit/materialize-deploy-key.sh` (`StrictHostKeyChecking yes`).
findings.finding: The plan provisions `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` as a GitHub repo var (S4) but does not require that the SSH client in the gerrit-verify workflow enforce `StrictHostKeyChecking=yes` against those pinned host keys. The existing codebase pattern (`service-user.sh`, `setup-replication.sh`, `reviewbot-e2e.sh`) uniformly uses `-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null`, which is acceptable only on private/internal network segments. The GitHub Actions → Gerrit SSH port 29418 vote path traverses the public internet; without explicit `StrictHostKeyChecking yes` and `UserKnownHostsFile` pointing at the materialized `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS`, the known-hosts var is decorative and the path is vulnerable to MITM (STRIDE: Spoofing of the Gerrit endpoint, leading to a fraudulent `Verified` vote or key exfiltration).
findings.location: S4 — Service account + credentials / S5 — The workflow (.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml)
findings.suggested_fix: In the S5 workflow spec, add a step before the SSH-based vote actions that writes `GERRIT_KNOWN_HOSTS` to a `~/.ssh/known_hosts` temp file and ensures the ssh config used by `gerrit-review-action` sets `StrictHostKeyChecking yes` and `UserKnownHostsFile` pointing to that file. Confirm `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` passes these SSH options (or wraps them via `GIT_SSH_COMMAND`/`SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS` env). Reference `materialize-deploy-key.sh` as the existing pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S4 (T5c — least-privilege / secret lifecycle):** Require that `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` is scoped to a named GitHub Actions environment (e.g., `gerrit-vote`) with deployment protection rules (branch restriction to `main` or required reviewer) rather than a bare repo-level secret, so the key is inaccessible to workflow files modified via unreviewed PRs on the public repo.
findings.finding: The `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` Actions secret grants SSH access to the Gerrit vote surface and is provisioned as a repo-level secret on a **public** GitHub repo with no GitHub Actions environment protection rule specified. On a public repo, any workflow file committed to `main` can reference repo-level secrets when triggered by `workflow_dispatch`. Since `gerrit-verify.yaml` is itself version-controlled in the repo, a PR that modifies it (merged by a collaborator) could introduce a step that exfiltrates the key during a subsequent g2p-dispatched run. The plan does not require gating secret access behind a GitHub Actions environment with deployment protection rules (e.g., required reviewers, branch restriction to `main`), which is the standard defense for sensitive secrets on public repos.
findings.location: S4 — Service account + credentials (Secrets & credentials section) / S5 — The workflow
findings.suggested_fix: In the S4 ADR and S5 workflow spec, add `environment: gerrit-vote` to the `vote` job in `gerrit-verify.yaml`, and document creating a GitHub Actions environment named `gerrit-vote` with 'Deployment branches: Selected branches → main'. Move `GERRIT_SSH_PRIVKEY` to that environment's secrets rather than the repo-level secret store. This ensures only workflow runs on `main`-dispatched refs can access the key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the rotation policy in S4: name the target document (e.g., a section in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` or an ops runbook), state the rotation interval (e.g., 90-day scheduled + immediate on compromise), and list the steps (revoke old secret, generate new, update SSM, redeploy container) so the criterion is verifiable by file inspection.
findings.finding: Secrets management in S4 says 'Define rotation + retire for both (compromise + PoC decommission)' without specifying the rotation interval, trigger events, or where the rotation runbook lives — deferring the rotation policy design to the executor.
findings.location: Secrets & credentials section + S4 AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S3 AC** — specify the required `gerrit_to_platform.ini` schema (section names, key names, PAT env-var reference, event-to-label/workflow mappings, and the `recheck → verify` comment-pattern key) alongside the processing rule that references it, so an executor can write the file from the plan alone.
findings.finding: The `recheck → verify` comment-triggered re-run rule is described in the Decision Log ('g2p `recheck` ChatOps') and referenced in S3 ('write `gerrit_to_platform.ini` … `recheck → verify` mapping'), but the `gerrit_to_platform.ini` configuration schema is never defined in the plan — required INI sections, keys, and the exact syntax for expressing the comment-to-event mapping are absent. The processing rule is thus specified without the schema it must be embedded in.
findings.location: Decision Log — 'Re-run: g2p `recheck` ChatOps'; S3 AC — 'write `gerrit_to_platform.ini` … `recheck → verify` mapping'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly split S3 into two sequential phases in its AC or add a note: 'Phase 1 (image build + plugin enable + hook symlinks) is independent of S4; Phase 2 (gerrit_to_platform.ini + coexistence proof) requires S4 to be complete first.'
findings.finding: S3 child states 'Depends on S2 (replication) + S4 (the PAT via the container-env secret path)', making S3 fully downstream of S4 for the `gerrit_to_platform.ini` step. The parent's deployment sequencing places S3 and S4 as parallel/paired ('S3/S4 (g2p + creds)'). This creates an unresolved ordering gap: a developer cannot complete S3's `gerrit_to_platform.ini` AC until S4 delivers the PAT in SSM, but nothing in either story explicitly sequences the fine-grained sub-steps.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to child S3 Scope: 'The image-build and plugin-enable steps (ACs 1–3) can proceed before S4; the ini configuration (AC 4) and coexistence proof (AC 5) require S4's PAT to be in SSM and fetch-secrets.sh to be updated.' This resolves the apparent contradiction with the parent's parallel sequencing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Agree on the canonical CI service-account group name (e.g., 'CI-Verified-Voters') and state it explicitly in both S1 and S4 acceptance criteria.
findings.finding: S1 must grant `label-Verified -1..+1` to a named CI service-account group in `project.config`, but neither S1 nor S4 specifies the canonical group name. S4 creates the group via `service-user.sh`; S1 writes the ACL referencing that group. Without an agreed name, the two children can silently diverge, leaving a dangling ACL reference in `project.config`.
findings.location: child 8d1b-f96a-4e3e-4eb2, child 2467-7f24-1204-4d15
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child S1 AC: 'The ACL grant uses group name CI-Verified-Voters (matching the group created by S4/service-user.sh).' Add to child S4 AC: 'service-user.sh creates the group named CI-Verified-Voters (matching the name used in S1/project.config).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in S6 which Terraform file (new, e.g., `infra/terraform/monitoring_s6.tf`, or existing `monitoring_s4b.tf`) will contain the new CloudWatch alarm resource for g2p and Verified-vote failures.
findings.finding: S6 child's CloudWatch alarm AC does not specify which Terraform file should be created or modified to define the alarm resource. The parent says the alarm is 'modelled on `monitoring_s4b.tf`' and the parent's 'New file consumers' list does not include a new monitoring `.tf` file — yet the alarm requires one. The child AC says only 'Add a CloudWatch alarm' and 'modeled on the `voter_errors` metric behind `monitoring_s4b.tf`' without naming the target file.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the S6 CloudWatch alarm AC: 'Create a new Terraform file `infra/terraform/monitoring_s6.tf` (or append to `monitoring_s4b.tf`) defining the CloudWatch log-metric filter and alarm for the g2p dispatch failure pattern and Verified-vote failure pattern.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S5 (or S3 coexistence probe), add a concrete measurement step: record actual replication lag for a sample patchset (e.g., time from `patchset-created` hook fire to `refs/changes/*` visible on GitHub), use that measurement to derive and document the sleep value, and commit the derived constant (or make it a workflow `env` variable) so it can be updated without hunting through inline YAML.
findings.finding: The `clear-vote` job's replication-lag sleep is specified only as 'a short sleep' with no value, no measurement plan, and no derivation from a spike or probe — making it an asserted threshold that could be too short (yielding races) or needlessly long (slowing CI) with no plan to validate it.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow / `clear-vote` job description
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-liner to the S3 coexistence probe: `time git ls-remote github-mirror refs/changes/XX/YY/ZZ` after pushing a test patchset; use the p95 of a few samples as the sleep value; set it as `env: REPLICATION_WAIT_SECS: <N>` at the top of `gerrit-verify.yaml`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the specific commit SHA or release tag of `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` where `vote-type: clear` is defined, and quote the relevant `action.yml` input enum or source line that confirms `clear → --label Verified=0` behavior, before finalising the S5 workflow design.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` accepts `vote-type: clear` (maps to `--label Verified=0`) as part of the verified vocabulary `clear|success|failure|cancelled`. No codebase artifact (no pinned action file, no `gerrit-verify.yaml` example, no README excerpt) corroborates this claim. The plan labels it a 'Verified fact (no longer open)' but provides no citation or hash to the action's source.
findings.location: Decision log — 'Verified facts'; S5 — The workflow (clear-vote job); Locked design — 3-job shape

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S3, specify that the hook symlink creation uses `ln -sf` (force-overwrite) or an idempotent guard (`[ -L $target ] || ln -s …`) so the entrypoint step is safe on container restart and g2p upgrades.
findings.finding: The symlink creation step ('at container start, symlink the three console-script hooks into the volume-mounted $site/hooks/') is not specified as idempotent. A plain `ln -s` command fails with EEXIST if the symlink already exists (e.g., container restart after initial setup), which would cause the container entrypoint to abort or require manual cleanup.
findings.location: S3 AC — 'at container start, symlink the three console-script hooks into the volume-mounted $site/hooks/ pointing at the in-container g2p paths'
findings.suggested_fix: In the entrypoint script: `for hook in patchset-created comment-added change-merged; do ln -sf "$(command -v g2p-${hook})" "${GERRIT_SITE}/hooks/${hook}"; done`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add S3 to S5's stated dependencies: 'End-to-end validation of the dispatch path depends on S3 (g2p operational in the container).'
findings.finding: S5 child lists its dependencies as 'Depends on S4 (the GitHub vars/secret) and references the `Verified` label from S1' but omits S3 as a dependency. The workflow file can be authored independently, but end-to-end testing and validation of the workflow (including the clear-vote and vote jobs exercising the SSH path) requires S3's g2p dispatch to be operational. Without this dependency noted, S5 may be closed as 'done' before it can be meaningfully tested.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend child S5 Scope to: 'Depends on S4 (the GitHub vars/secret) and S1 (the Verified label); end-to-end dispatch validation also requires S3. Out of scope: g2p host install (S3), the gate activation (S6).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the full output vocabulary of `im-open/workflow-conclusion` and add an explicit mapping step (e.g., a `run: echo conclusion=$CONCLUSION` + conditional) that normalises any out-of-vocabulary value to `failure` before passing to `gerrit-review-action vote-type`, and document this mapping in S5.
findings.finding: The plan passes `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` (output of `im-open/workflow-conclusion`) directly as `vote-type` to `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`. The plan only documents three values (`success`, `failure`, `cancelled`) but `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can emit additional conclusion values (e.g., `skipped`, `timed_out`, `stale`, `neutral`). If `gerrit-review-action` receives an undocumented `vote-type` value it may error or silently no-op, leaving `Verified=0` (fail-closed is fine) but with no clear error surfaced.
findings.location: Locked design — 3-job shape (vote job); S5 — The workflow
findings.suggested_fix: Add a workflow step between `im-open/workflow-conclusion` and `gerrit-review-action` that maps `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` to `success|failure|cancelled`: any value not in `{success}` → `failure`; `cancelled` passes through. This is the pattern used in O-RAN-SC's `gerrit-required-verify.yaml`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S5** — Before finalizing the workflow, verify the full output vocabulary of `im-open/workflow-conclusion` against its source/docs, confirm which values `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` accepts as `vote-type`, and add an explicit mapping step (e.g., a `run: echo` step or `if` conditional) that collapses any unexpected conclusion values to `failure` before passing to `gerrit-review-action`.
findings.finding: The plan passes `im-open/workflow-conclusion`'s output directly as `vote-type` to `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action` and asserts the vocabulary mapping is `success→+1`, `failure|cancelled→-1`. However, `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can output additional values beyond these four — including `skipped`, `timed_out`, and `startup_failure` — none of which appear in the stated `vote-type` vocabulary (`clear|success|failure|cancelled`). What `gerrit-review-action` does when it receives an unrecognized `vote-type` (error-out, silently cast 0, etc.) is unspecified and unverified.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow; Locked design 'vote job'; Key references (`im-open/workflow-conclusion`, `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a mapping step between `im-open/workflow-conclusion` and `gerrit-review-action`: `conclusion=${{ steps.conclusion.outputs.workflow_conclusion }}; [[ "$conclusion" == 'success' ]] || conclusion='failure'; echo "VOTE_TYPE=$conclusion" >> $GITHUB_ENV` — then use `vote-type: ${{ env.VOTE_TYPE }}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S5 — workflow** `gerrit-verify.yaml`: define the complete `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` → `vote-type` mapping table (all values `im-open/workflow-conclusion` can emit) and add an explicit guard/mapping step that collapses any non-`success` value to `failure` before passing to `gerrit-review-action`, rather than piping the raw conclusion directly.
findings.finding: The `WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION` enum produced by `im-open/workflow-conclusion` is piped directly as `vote-type` into `lfreleng-actions/gerrit-review-action`, but the plan only names two branches ('success'→+1, 'failure|cancelled'→−1). The full output vocabulary of `im-open/workflow-conclusion` (which can include 'skipped', 'timed_out', 'neutral', 'stale', 'startup_failure') is never defined, and no guard or mapping rule is specified for unrecognized values — leaving it undefined what vote is cast (or whether the action errors) when the conclusion is, e.g., 'timed_out'.
findings.location: Locked design — S5 workflow shape; `vote` job: `gerrit-review-action vote-type: ${{ WORKFLOW_CONCLUSION }}`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a mapping step between `im-open/workflow-conclusion` and `gerrit-review-action`: `vote_type=$([[ "$CONCLUSION" == 'success' ]] && echo 'success' || echo 'failure')` — treating any non-success conclusion (timed_out, skipped, neutral, stale, cancelled, etc.) as failure to maintain fail-closed semantics.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename child S3 title to 'S3 — Install gerrit-to-platform in the Gerrit container' to match the story body and parent decision.
findings.finding: S3 child ticket title says 'Install gerrit-to-platform on the Gerrit host' but the entire story — Why, What, Scope, and every AC — consistently and correctly specifies the Gerrit **container**. The word 'host' in the title directly contradicts the story's core premise and the parent's explicitly-decided in-container runtime placement.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the ticket title from 'Install gerrit-to-platform on the Gerrit host' to 'Install gerrit-to-platform in the Gerrit container'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in both S1 and S4 that the ACL grant lives exclusively in `project.config` (S1 owns it) and `service-user.sh` (S4) only creates the group — and agree on the canonical group name in both children.
findings.finding: The `label-Verified -1..+1` ACL grant has dual ownership: the parent's S4 acceptance criterion assigns it to `service-user.sh` ('account + group + `Verified` ACL'), while child S1 also claims it (in `project.config`) and child S4 requalifies it as 'from S1' — creating an inconsistency between the parent S4 AC and both children. It is unclear whether `service-user.sh` independently sets the ACL (e.g., via `gerrit set-project-config`) or only creates the group that S1's `project.config` grant references.
findings.location: child 8d1b-f96a-4e3e-4eb2, child 2467-7f24-1204-4d15
findings.suggested_fix: In child S4, remove 'ACL' from the service-user.sh AC scope and state explicitly: 'service-user.sh creates the CI service account and its group (e.g., CI-Verified-Voters); the label-Verified ACL grant referencing this group is authored in S1 (project.config).' In child S1, name the group explicitly (e.g., 'CI-Verified-Voters') so S4 can create the group with the exact same name.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve core-plugin ownership: either move the `hooks`/`replication` plugin-enablement deliverable into S2's AC (so Decomposition and AC align at the S2 tier) or update the Decomposition to read 'S2 (replication) is earliest; S3 (g2p install, which includes core plugin enablement) depends on S2' — removing the ambiguous 'core plugins' grouping at the S2 level.
findings.finding: The Decomposition section places core plugin enablement at the same tier as S2 ('S2 + core plugins are earliest'), but the S3 AC is where that deliverable lives; the Deployment Sequencing's parenthetical 'S2 (replication) + core plugins (S3)' tries to bridge both but is ambiguous — an implementer reading the Decomposition may attempt to deliver core plugins as part of S2 work, causing a story-boundary conflict.
findings.location: Decomposition section ('S2 + core plugins are earliest') vs. S3 AC ('enable the hooks + replication core plugins') vs. Deployment sequencing ('S2 (replication) + core plugins (S3)')
findings.suggested_fix: Update Decomposition to: 'S2 (replication broadening) is earliest; S3 (g2p install + core plugin enablement) depends on S2; S4 pairs with S3; …' and update Deployment Sequencing to: 'S2 → S3 (g2p + core plugins) + S4 (creds) → S5 → prove voter → S1 activates (S6 E2E)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **E3 — test.yml convergence decision:** Resolve the 'decide' deferral in S5 by explicitly stating whether `test.yml` is (a) left unchanged and `gerrit-verify.yaml` reuses its jobs via `workflow_call`, (b) modified in-place to add `workflow_dispatch` inputs, or (c) replaced by a hybrid file — and acknowledge the impact on existing branch-push/manual callers of `test.yml`.
findings.finding: The plan defers the test.yml convergence decision to S5 ('Decide test.yml convergence — recommend the gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml hybrid so one file serves Gerrit dispatch + manual/branch runs') but does not acknowledge how the existing test.yml callers (branch pushes, manual runs, any other workflow triggers) are affected if test.yml is modified into a hybrid format or replaced.
findings.location: S5 — The workflow `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the Gerrit `hooks` server plugin is present and activatable in the `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image (inspect the WAR or a running container for `WEB-INF/plugins/hooks.jar`), then add it to `plugins/README.md` with its enablement method (init flag, copy path, or note that it is already-loaded), matching the existing plugin-discipline pattern.
findings.finding: The plan's `plugins/README.md` and `install-plugins.sh` document only `webhooks`, `events-log`, and `oauth` — the Gerrit `hooks` server plugin (which g2p requires to dispatch `patchset-created`/`comment-added`/`change-merged`) appears nowhere in any codebase artifact. The plan claims `hooks` is a 'core plugin bundled in gerrit.war, enabled via init --install-plugin or copy-from-war; NO external download/sha256 pin' — but this is unverified against the actual `gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1` image inventory, and the existing plugin discipline (README + install-plugins.sh) has no entry for it.
findings.location: S3 — g2p install; Decision log 'Verified facts'; infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md; infra/gerrit/install-plugins.sh
findings.suggested_fix: Run `docker run --rm gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1 ls /var/gerrit/plugins/ /var/gerrit/lib/` and also `unzip -l gerrit.war | grep hooks` inside the image to confirm presence; update `plugins/README.md` with the `hooks` entry (bundled, no download) before committing to S3.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
